SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.java.transformations.pipeline

Class JavaTransformer

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.transformations.pipeline.JavaTransformer
Direct Known Subclasses:
ClassInitializeMethodBuilder, ClassPreparationMethodBuilder, ConstantStringExpressionEvaluator, ConstructorNormalformBuilder, CreateBuilder, CreateObjectBuilder, EnumClassBuilder, ImplicitFieldAdder, InstanceAllocationMethodBuilder, JMLCommentTransformer, JmlDocRemoval, JMLTransformer, LocalClassTransformation, PrepareObjectBuilder
@NullMarked public abstract class JavaTransformer extends Object
The JavaDL requires some implicit fields, that are available in each Java class. The name of the implicit fields starts usually with a dollar sign. (in the age of recoder it was enclosed in angle brackets).

To access the fields in a uniform way, they are added as usual fields to the classes, in particular this allows us to parse them in easier. For further information see also

  • ImplicitFieldAdder
  • CreateObjectBuilder
  • PrepareObjectBuilder

Performance of these classes was low, so information that is shared between all instances of a transformation set has been outsourced to a transformation cache.

  • Field Details

    • services

      protected final TransformationPipelineServices services
      Further services and helper function for this pipeline step.

    • cache

      protected final TransformationPipelineServices.TransformerCache cache
      a cache object that stores information which is needed by and common to many transformations. it includes the compilation units, the declared classes, and information for local classes.

  • Constructor Details

    • JavaTransformer

      public JavaTransformer(TransformationPipelineServices services)
      creates a transformer for the recoder model
      Parameters:
      services - the CrossReferenceServiceConfiguration to access model information

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public void apply(com.github.javaparser.ast.body.TypeDeclaration<?> td)
      The method is called for each type declaration of the compilation unit and initiates the syntactical transformation. If you want to descend in inner classes you have to implement the recursion by yourself.

    • apply

      public void apply(com.github.javaparser.ast.CompilationUnit cu)

    • reportError

      public static RuntimeException reportError(com.github.javaparser.ast.Node node, String message, Object... args)
